title: Timeout Layers, Deadlines, Cancellation, Cleanup, and Budget Allocation · Browser and Node… · Inference Brief: Caller Deadline Propagation
summary: A search-as-you-type interface cancels stale requests with composed AbortSignals while preserving the winning result and announced status. Planned artifact: Add and verify the http ts caller deadline propagation increment in a TypeScript 7 standards-based Fetch end-to-end request budget and cancellation model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Propagate caller cancellation and deadlines through request creation, helpers, parsing, retries, and downstream work. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.

Separate name resolution, connect, TLS, response-header, body-read, idle, and whole-operation timeout controls. Demonstrate the result through TypeScript 7 strict types plus WHATWG Fetch Request, Response, Headers, URL, streams, and AbortSignal contracts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.
